People

Principal Investigator

Prof. Dr. Florian Haselbeck

Professor & Group Leader

Prof. Dr. Florian Haselbeck leads the smAIL-WS at the Department of Sustainable Agriculture and Energy Systems, Hochschule Weihenstephan-Triesdorf. His research focuses on applied artificial intelligence and digitalization in agriculture to address global challenges in food production and sustainability.

Postdoctoral Researchers

Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi

Postdoctoral Researcher

Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi is a postdoctoral researcher with expertise in Computer Vision and CrossModal data analysis, specializing in Animal Monitoring systems. His research leverages multi-modal sensor data to develop intelligent solutions for livestock farming and animal welfare assessment.

Dr. Georg Back

Postdoctoral Researcher

Dr. Georg Back is a postdoctoral researcher with expertise in biology and agricultural systems. His research focuses on understanding genotype-phenotype relationships using deep learning approaches to improve plant breeding and agricultural productivity.

PhD Students

Harshavardhan Subramanian

PhD Student

Harshavardhan Subramanian is a PhD student working on weed detection using computer vision and machine learning techniques. His research focuses on leveraging large pre-trained foundation models to develop generalizable weed detection methods for sustainable agriculture.

Leonie Hahn

PhD Student

Leonie Hahn is a PhD student working on the smartReForest project, developing smart solutions for resilient reforestation to secure essential ecosystem services in the context of climate change. Her research combines AI and environmental science to address forestry challenges.

Marco Obermeier

PhD Student

Marco Obermeier is a PhD student working on the smartBattery project, applying machine learning and optimization techniques for optimal integration of battery storage in smart grids. His research focuses on data-driven methods and machine learning for energy engineering – including time series analysis, forecasting, and optimization – to enhance renewable energy integration in power grids.

Lina-Liv Willruth

PhD Student

Lina-Liv Willruth is a PhD student working on the prevention of plant diseases in hop production, focusing on modernizing the outdated downy mildew warning system. Her research leverages artificial intelligence and smart trapping technologies to partly automate disease monitoring and develop a regionalized early warning system. By integrating data-driven models with field-based sensing, she aims to improve prediction accuracy, support timely interventions, and enhance sustainable crop protection strategies.

Student Assistants

TBD

TBD

TBD is a student assistant supporting the research group with various projects and administrative tasks, contributing to the advancement of smart farming technologies.
